reason: The analytical fallback branch (lines 419-436 of scattering_bp.jl, triggered when d > 50) has a constraint-counting bug: it uses input-space lambda equality as a proxy for total J_z inequality, overcounting some inter-block constraints and missing all intra-block (ia==ja) constraints. For Jmax=13 (d=108), this gives r_BP=107 instead of the correct 109. This does NOT affect any values currently in the paper table (all four rows have d <= 50 and use the SDP path), but would silently produce wrong bounds if someone extends the table to larger Jmax. Suggested fix: replace the analytical n_cons calculation with the full (ia,a,ja,b) loop used in the SDP path.
